name: kunilingus-bridge
description: HTTP bridge for OpenClaw bots (Telegram/WhatsApp) to control GitHub Copilot agents in VS Code — vibe code from your phone

kunilingus-bridge.port.description: Port for the HTTP bridge server

kunilingus-bridge.autoStart.description: Automatically start the bridge server on VS Code startup

kunilingus-bridge.apiKey.description: Optional API key to protect the bridge (leave empty to disable)

kunilingus-bridge.defaultModel.description: Model family override. Leave empty to auto-select whatever Copilot model is available.

kunilingus-bridge.webhookUrl.description: URL where the bridge POSTs results back to your bot (OpenClaw). Leave empty to disable webhook callbacks.

kunilingus-bridge.webhookSecret.description: HMAC-SHA256 secret for signing webhook payloads (X-Bridge-Signature header). Leave empty to skip signing.

kunilingus-bridge.messagingPlatform.description: Target messaging platform — affects response formatting (Markdown for Telegram, plain text for WhatsApp).

kunilingus-bridge.maxResponseTokens.description: Max tokens for LLM responses. Lower = cheaper. 0 = unlimited.

kunilingus-bridge.summarizeForMessaging.description: Auto-summarize long responses before sending via webhook to messaging platforms.

kunilingus-bridge.summaryMaxChars.description: Maximum characters for auto-summaries sent to Telegram/WhatsApp.

kunilingus-bridge.bindAddress.description: Network address to bind the server. Use 0.0.0.0 for remote access (always set apiKey when exposing!).

scripts.pretest: npm run compile && npm run lint
